title: curio.frontmatter.v1
description: YAML frontmatter carried by every markdown note Curio exports into a named destination, expressed over the YAML data model as JSON. `curio_id` is identity (UUIDv7, assigned at save, never changes); `checksum` is a change token ONLY and covers exactly the bytes of the managed content region (between `<!-- curio:managed:begin v1 -->` and `<!-- curio:managed:end -->`), never the whole file. Consumers MUST NOT use checksum as identity. User-added frontmatter keys unknown to Curio are preserved on re-export, so additional properties are permitted. The per-destination export-idempotency manifest (`.curio/manifest.json`, `curio.manifest.v1`) is published under `$defs/manifest` in this file.

curio_id.description: Article identity: UUIDv7, assigned at save, never changes. An edited article keeps its id.

feed.description: Feed URL the article came from; null if the article was saved manually. The key is always present.

published.description: Publication time, RFC 3339 UTC (`Z`-suffixed, fractional seconds optional); null if unknown. The key is always present.

saved.description: Time the article was saved in Curio. RFC 3339 UTC with exactly millisecond precision.

tags.description: Curio-assigned tags at export time. May be empty.

checksum.description: sha256 of the managed content region bytes. Change token ONLY — never identity: two articles can have identical content, and an edited article keeps its curio_id while the checksum changes.

word_count.description: Word count of the extracted article. Optional.

curioId.description: UUIDv7 in canonical lowercase hyphenated form (version nibble 7, RFC 4122 variant).

checksum.description: SHA-256 digest in lowercase hex with the literal `sha256:` prefix.

timestampMillis.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with exactly millisecond precision, `Z`-suffixed (e.g. 2026-07-03T09:15:00.123Z).

timestampUtc.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp, `Z`-suffixed, fractional seconds optional (e.g. 2026-07-01T12:00:00Z).

manifest.title: curio.manifest.v1
manifest.description: Per-destination export-idempotency oracle at `.curio/manifest.json`, mapping curio_id to export record. On disk the writer keeps keys sorted, 2-space indent, one entry per line (git-mergeable diffs), and writes the note file first, the manifest second, via temp-file + atomic rename — a crash leaves an orphan note, never a dangling manifest entry. Export is idempotent on (curio_id, checksum): unchanged means no write. Consumers read the manifest to learn write-ownership: paths present here are Curio-owned at the managed-region level.

notes.description: Map from curio_id to export record. Keys are sorted on disk.

manifestEntry.description: Export record for one note in this destination.

path.description: Note path relative to the destination root.

checksum.description: Checksum of the managed region as last exported. Change token ONLY, never identity.

exported_at.description: Time of the last export of this note. RFC 3339 UTC with exactly millisecond precision.
